request: The turnstile puzzle seems broken now; there's no lever, and it doesn't stop you from advancing north

messages.content:
<player-context>
Player "player:1" is in "room:gate" ("The Gate").
</player-context>

<current-room>
{
  "id": "room:gate",
  "tags": [
    "room"
  ],
  "name": "The Gate",
  "description": "A worn stone archway marks the edge of the market. The stones are smooth from centuries of hands brushing them for luck on the way in — a tradition that predates any living vendor and which nobody has ever verified actually works. Beyond the arch, the noise hits you. A heavy brass turnstile, once immovable, now stands rotated to one side. It moves with a sound like a choir of saws. Nearby, a heap of junk has accumulated against the wall. A faded sign bolted to the archway reads TINKERMARKET in letters made from dozens of different metals.",
  "location": "region:market",
  "containedBy": [
    "region:market",
    "world"
  ],
  "secret": "The sign letters are each made from a different dimensional metal. One letter — the K — is made from a metal nobody at the market can identify anymore. A void glass shard vibrates strongly near it. Tiv has been trying to buy the K for years.",
  "room": {
    "darkWhenUnlit": false,
    "visits": 0
  },
  "children": [
    {
      "id": "exit:gate:north",
      "tags": [
        "exit"
      ],

        "region:market",
        "world"
      ],

        "destination": "room:market-square-south"

      }
    },
    {

    },
    {
      "id": "item:junk-pile",
      "tags": [
        "fixed-display"
      ],
      "name": "heap of junk",
      "description": "A heap of discarded dimensional shipping crates and bent metal. It's the kind of pile that accumulates when nobody is quite sure if something is trash or a rare component. Someone has thoughtfully added a sign that says NOT FOR SCAVENGING, which has been largely ignored. A rusty lever pokes out from the mess.",
      "location": "room:gate",
      "containedBy": [
        "room:gate",
        "region:market",
        "world"
      ],
      "secret": "The pile contains a few useful bits of scrap. This is a common occurrence at the market — one person's junk is another's critical spare part.",
      "properties": {
        "fixed": true
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "npc:kip",
      "tags": [
        "npc",
        "talkable"
      ],
      "name": "Kip",
      "description": "An elderly vendor with skin like parchment who sits on a stool by the gate, watching the traffic with an expression of profound boredom. He wears a vest with too many pockets and a hat that might have once been a different color. He seems to be the unofficial gatekeeper, or at least the person most likely to complain if you do something wrong.",
      "location": "room:gate",
      "containedBy": [
        "room:gate",
        "region:market",
        "world"
      ],
      "secret": "Kip has been sitting here for forty years. He doesn't work for the market; he just likes to watch the people. He knows the turnstile is broken and enjoys watching newcomers figure it out. If asked, he'll mention that 'the last fellow who tried to force it ended up with a very sore shoulder and a very intact turnstile.'",
      "properties": {
        "origin": "The Dust Plains"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "item:stuck-turnstile",
      "tags": [
        "fixed-display",
        "turnstile"
      ],
      "name": "rotated turnstile",
      "description": "The brass turnstile is now rotated, clearing the way north. It groans occasionally, as if offended by the sudden movement.",

        "region:market",
        "world"
      ],

      "properties": {
        "material": "brass",
        "fixed": true
      }
    }
  ],

        "description": "The quieter end of the square, where the crowd thins near the gate. A wooden notice board bristles with pinned papers — requests, offers, warnings in scripts you can't read. A stone bench sits against a low wall, its surface polished by years of use. Nearby, a vendor tends a brass cart of roasted nuts that smell like cinnamon. The square opens up to the north.",
        "location": "region:market",
        "containedBy": [
          "region:market",
          "world"
        ],
        "secret": "The nut vendor's cart runs on a heat source from the same dimension as the forge fuel. The sharper smell is a spice that mildly enhances perception for about ten minutes after eating — if a player buys and eats the nuts, descriptions of materials they examine should include subtle hints about the material's properties. Regulars know this and snack here before browsing.",

              "region:market",

            "exit": {

            },
            "destinationName": "Market Square"
          },
          {
            "id": "exit:market-square-south:east",
            "tags": [
              "exit"
            ],
            "name": "The Nut Cart",
            "description": "A brass cart tended by a vendor with too many elbows. Smells good.",
            "location": "room:market-square-south",
            "containedBy": [
              "room:market-square-south",
              "region:market",
              "world"
            ],
            "exit": {
              "direction": "east",

            "destinationName": "The Nut Cart"
          },
          {
            "id": "exit:market-square-south:west",
            "tags": [
              "exit"
            ],
            "name": "The Lens Grinder",
            "description": "A workbench under a canvas awning, covered in glass dust.",
            "location": "room:market-square-south",
            "containedBy": [

            "description": "A weathered wooden board bristling with pinned papers. Requests for rare materials, offers of services, warnings about unreliable vendors, a few sketched maps of market sections. Most are in scripts you can't read, but some have been helpfully illustrated. One recurring notice, pinned in several places, reads: BRING FINISHED GOODS TO THE COLLECTOR — FAIR PRICES PAID.",
            "location": "room:market-square-south",
            "containedBy": [
              "room:market-square-south",
              "region:market",
              "world"
            ],
            "aliases": [
              "board",
              "notices",
              "papers"
            ],
            "secret": "Some of the notices are written in a script that changes when you're not looking directly at it. One particular notice, pinned under several layers of others, is a map of the market that shows stalls and passages that don't seem to exist yet — or don't exist anymore.",
            "scenery": [
              {
                "word": "maps",
                "aliases": [
                  "ink",
                  "vellum",
                  "back-alleys",
                  "sketches"
                ],
                "description": "The maps are hand-drawn on scraps of vellum and dried leaf, showing the market as a shifting cluster of circles and overlapping planes. One particular map depicts a series of back-alleys that appear to connect the nut vendor's cart directly to a distant forge. The ink on that one seems to migrate across the page if you blink too fast.",
                "rejection": "The map is pinned firmly behind several layers of more recent commerce."
              }
            ],
            "properties": {
              "fixed": true,
              "takeRefusal": "The board is bolted to the wall."
            }
          }
        ]

}

result.name: The Gate
result.description: A worn stone archway marks the edge of the market. The stones are smooth from centuries of hands brushing them for luck on the way in — a tradition that predates any living vendor and which nobody has ever verified actually works. Beyond the arch, the noise hits you. A heavy brass turnstile, once immovable, now stands rotated to one side. It moves with a sound like a choir of saws. Nearby, a heap of junk has accumulated against the wall. A faded sign bolted to the archway reads TINKERMARKET in letters made from dozens of different metals.
result.secret: The sign letters are each made from a different dimensional metal. One letter — the K — is made from a metal nobody at the market can identify anymore. A void glass shard vibrates strongly near it. Tiv has been trying to buy the K for years.

children.name: Into the Market
children.description: The archway opens onto the south end of the market square. The noise and bustle grow louder ahead.

children.name: heap of junk
children.description: A heap of discarded dimensional shipping crates and bent metal. It's the kind of pile that accumulates when nobody is quite sure if something is trash or a rare component. Someone has thoughtfully added a sign that says NOT FOR SCAVENGING, which has been largely ignored. A rusty lever pokes out from the mess.
children.secret: The pile contains a few useful bits of scrap. This is a common occurrence at the market — one person's junk is another's critical spare part.

children.name: Kip
children.description: An elderly vendor with skin like parchment who sits on a stool by the gate, watching the traffic with an expression of profound boredom. He wears a vest with too many pockets and a hat that might have once been a different color. He seems to be the unofficial gatekeeper, or at least the person most likely to complain if you do something wrong.
children.secret: Kip has been sitting here for forty years. He doesn't work for the market; he just likes to watch the people. He knows the turnstile is broken and enjoys watching newcomers figure it out. If asked, he'll mention that 'the last fellow who tried to force it ended up with a very sore shoulder and a very intact turnstile.'

children.name: rotated turnstile
children.description: The brass turnstile is now rotated, clearing the way north. It groans occasionally, as if offended by the sudden movement.
children.secret: The turnstile is part of a series of security measures that haven't been maintained since the last Great Reorganization. It requires a simple lever to function. Any long, sturdy rod will do, but the one in the junk pile is the most convenient.

result.name: handheld abrasive lance
result.description: A heavy tool connected to a thick, reinforced hose. It features a brass nozzle that feels cold from the high-velocity flow inside, and a safety lever that looks like it requires a firm grip. The nozzle is caked with dried garnet grit.
result.secret: If the player uses this on a hard material while carrying magnetic grit, the grit will be sucked into the stream, allowing the lance to cut through nearly anything. Without an abrasive additive, it just washes things very aggressively.

result.name: rotated turnstile
result.description: The brass turnstile is now rotated, clearing the way north. It groans occasionally, as if offended by the sudden movement.
result.secret: The turnstile is part of a series of security measures that haven't been maintained since the last Great Reorganization. It requires a simple lever to function. Any long, sturdy rod will do, but the one in the junk pile is the most convenient.

result.description: A heap of discarded dimensional shipping crates and bent metal. It's the kind of pile that accumulates when nobody is quite sure if something is trash or a rare component. Someone has thoughtfully added a sign that says NOT FOR SCAVENGING, which has been largely ignored. A rusty lever pokes out from the mess.
result.secret: The pile contains a few useful bits of scrap. This is a common occurrence at the market — one person's junk is another's critical spare part.

create.name: rusty lever
create.description: A long, heavy bar of iron with a grip at one end. It’s pitted with rust but still feels sturdy enough to move a mountain, or at least a very stubborn piece of machinery.
create.secret: This lever was once part of the gate's original mechanism. It's the only thing in this pile that actually fits the turnstile's socket. It smells faintly of old grease and ozone.

value.name: stuck turnstile
value.description: A heavy brass turnstile blocks the path north. Its gears are visible through a gap in the casing, and there's a distinct socket where a handle or lever should be. It looks like it hasn't moved since the last era.

A worn stone archway marks the edge of the market. The stones are smooth from centuries of hands brushing them for luck on the way in — a tradition that predates any living vendor and which nobody has ever verified actually works. Beyond the arch, the noise hits you. A heavy brass turnstile stands firmly in the way, blocking the passage north. Nearby, a heap of junk has accumulated against the wall. A faded sign bolted to the archway reads TINKERMARKET in letters made from dozens of different metals.

input.summary: Reset the turnstile puzzle in The Gate by recreating the missing rusty lever, locking the northward exit, and updating the room and turnstile descriptions to reflect their stuck state. Created 'item:rusty-lever' with the 'turnstile-fix' tag to ensure compatibility with existing verb handlers.

summary: Reset the turnstile puzzle in The Gate by recreating the missing rusty lever, locking the northward exit, and updating the room and turnstile descriptions to reflect their stuck state. Created 'item:rusty-lever' with the 'turnstile-fix' tag to ensure compatibility with existing verb handlers.
